title: Dead Code, Unused Dependencies, and Reachability · Failure Case: Unreachable Code
summary: Remove unreachable code and dependencies with evidence rather than guesswork. Planned artifact: Add and verify the gates unreachable code increment in a dead-code and dependency audit. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Detect unreachable branches, impossible states, and obsolete feature paths.
Treat unused imports, variables, parameters, private members, and exports according to language semantics.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# Risk-profiled repository gate portfolio
# Each gate needs purpose, command, failure fixture, owner, and suppression policy.

steps.title: Predict Unreachable Code
steps.instruction:
Use a changed theory case derived from this module: gates dead code. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result.

steps.title: Build Unreachable Code evidence
steps.instruction: Append a gates-unreachable-code-100jmd: manifest entry with concrete verify plus failure, owner, or repair evidence.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Unreachable Code into observable performance evidence.

content.code:
gates-unreachable-code-100jmd:
  verify: [replace with a deterministic check]
  failure: [replace with the rejected condition]
  repair: [replace with a local reproduction and correction path]
content.caption: Shape of the required increment; replace generic language with the current stakeholder case.

steps.title: Defend the Unreachable Code model
steps.instruction: Write a concise evidence note that explains when Unreachable Code applies, what can fail, and how the changed case was verified.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Detect unreachable branches, impossible states, and obsolete feature paths.
